analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-318

POL-318.title: IMPLEMENT: Decouple packets from tracker assumptions with spec-to-work-contract intake
POL-318.body:
## Objective

Introduce a normalized Work Contract abstraction so Polaris can accept work from any source (Linear, spec file, GitHub issue, prompt) and execute it without requiring a tracker.

## Context

Created from analysis of [POL-317](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-317/analyze-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Current packet generation assumes work originates from a Linear or tracker-backed issue. This cluster removes all tracker-specific coupling from the runtime intake, packet compiler, and finalize paths and introduces a Work Contract normalization layer. See [POL-317](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-317/analyze-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work) for full analysis and architecture.

## Goal

After this cluster completes:

* `polaris run` works with a local spec file and no tracker configured.
* `polaris analyze spec <path>` produces local child work units without creating Linear issues.
* Packet compilers and skill packet generators produce tracker-agnostic text.
* Finalize fully skips all tracker steps when no tracker is configured.
* A `WorkContract` type normalizes work from all sources before packet generation.

## Scope

* src/types/
* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/defaults.ts
* src/loop/run-preflight.ts
* src/loop/worker-packet.ts
* src/skill-packet/generator.ts
* src/tracker/adapters/
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/11-update-linear.ts
* src/cli/
* docs/Polaris/spec/
* src/\*\*/\*.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `WorkContract` interface exists and is used by packet compilers.
* `polaris run` can bootstrap from a local spec without a tracker.
* `compileFinalizePacket` step text is tracker-conditional.
* `buildAnalyzePacket` allowed_outputs contains no hardcoded "Linear" text.
* Finalize skips tracker steps gracefully when `tracker.adapter` is unset.
* Unit tests cover WorkContract intake from spec and tracker sources.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

None. This is a standalone cluster.

## Non-goals

* Removing Linear support.
* Replacing the MCP bridge adapter.
* Changing the worker dispatch protocol.

POL-324.title: Write work-contract spec doc and add integration tests for trackerless execution
POL-324.body:
## Objective

Document the Work Contract architecture in a canonical spec document and add integration tests that verify trackerless bootstrap and dispatch round-trips.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). This is the terminal child — it documents what children 1–5 built and verifies the full trackerless execution path works end-to-end.

## Goal

1. Write `docs/Polaris/spec/work-contract-spec.md` documenting:
   * The Work Contract normalized interface (`WorkContract` type fields and semantics).
   * How each intake source (tracker, spec, prompt) maps to a WorkContract.
   * The execution flow: source → WorkContract → LocalGraph → bootstrap → dispatch → finalize.
   * Trackerless vs. tracker-backed mode differences.
   * How evidence, commits, validation, and closeout work without a tracker.
2. Add unit tests in `src/tracker/adapters/spec/index.test.ts` for SpecAdapter with a fixture spec markdown file.
3. Add integration test in `src/loop/run-bootstrap.test.ts` (or a new file) that exercises trackerless bootstrap: create a spec, run SpecAdapter.syncIn(), call runLoopBootstrapInit(), verify the resulting state has correct cluster_id and open_children.

## Scope

* docs/Polaris/spec/work-contract-spec.md
* src/tracker/adapters/spec/index.test.ts
* src/loop/bootstrap-trackerless.integration.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `docs/Polaris/spec/work-contract-spec.md` exists and covers all 7 points above.
* `SpecAdapter` unit tests pass with fixture spec file.
* Trackerless bootstrap integration test passes.
* `npm run build` passes.
* `npm test` passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/tracker/adapters/spec
* npx vitest run src/loop/bootstrap-trackerless

## Ordering

* Blocked by child 4 (SpecAdapter must exist) and child 5 (finalize trackerless support must be in place).

## Non-goals

* Do not add end-to-end finalize tests in this child (that is a separate test cluster).
* Do not document GitHub issue or prompt sources (out of scope for this cluster).

POL-323.title: Ensure finalize is fully trackerless-capable when no tracker adapter is configured
POL-323.body:
## Objective

Audit and harden `runFinalize()` so that every tracker-dependent step skips cleanly when no tracker adapter is configured or when `tracker.adapter === "local"`.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Finalize already has partial guards — step 6 checks `config.tracker?.adapter` and step 13 checks `linearEnabled`. However, two gaps remain:

1. Step 13 (`stepUpdateLinear`) uses `state.cluster_id` directly as the Linear issue ID. For trackerless runs, `cluster_id` is a local slug or spec path — passing it to a Linear API call would silently fail or produce a wrong update. The guard is present but the intent could be clearer.
2. Step 6 logs "Tracker not configured" but does not distinguish between `adapter === "local"` (intentionally local) and `adapter === undefined` (not yet configured). These should produce different log messages.
3. The finalize worker packet instruction step 7 (addressed in child 3) and the finalize step log header still say "Updating Linear..." even when Linear is not configured — this will be fixed here for the runtime log messages.

## Goal

1. In `src/finalize/index.ts` step 6: add a branch for `adapter === "local"` that logs `"[6/14] Local adapter — skipping remote tracker reconciliation."`.
2. In `src/finalize/index.ts` step 13: rename the log message from `"Updating Linear..."` to `"Updating tracker (if configured)..."` and add a note when skipping why it was skipped.
3. In `src/finalize/steps/11-update-linear.ts`: add a log line specifying the issue ID being used and confirming it is a Linear ID (guard against using a spec-path as a Linear issue ID).
4. Add `tracker.adapter === "local"` to the `linearEnabled` resolution so it always evaluates to `false` when local.

## Scope

* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/11-update-linear.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `adapter === "local"` produces a distinct log message in step 6.
* Step 13 log says "Updating tracker (if configured)..." instead of "Updating Linear...".
* `stepUpdateLinear` is never called when `adapter === "local"`.
* All existing finalize tests pass.
* `npm run build` passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize

## Ordering

* Blocked by child 1 (PolarisConfig must have `adapter: "local"` in the union before this can compile).

## Non-goals

* Do not change the tracker reconciliation logic for `linear` or `mcp-bridge` adapters.
* Do not change finalize PR creation or commit logic.

POL-322.title: Implement spec-to-contract intake adapter and wire polaris analyze/run spec CLI commands
POL-322.body:
## Objective

Create a SpecAdapter that reads a markdown spec file and produces a LocalGraph, and wire `polaris analyze spec <path>` and `polaris run spec <path>` CLI entry points to use it.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Currently there is no way to invoke `polaris run` or `polaris analyze` on a local spec file — both commands require a Linear issue ID. A `SpecAdapter` reads a structured markdown file (with `## Objective`, `## Scope`, `## Validation`, `## Children` sections) and produces an `ExecutionGraphV2` with `source.type: "spec"`, allowing the rest of the runtime (bootstrap, dispatch, finalize) to operate without a tracker.

## Goal

1. Create `src/tracker/adapters/spec/index.ts` with a `SpecAdapter` class that implements `syncIn(specPath)` and returns a `LocalGraph`. The adapter reads the canonical section format from the spec file and maps sections to `ExecutionNode` fields.
2. Create `src/tracker/adapters/local/index.ts` with a `LocalAdapter` that wraps an already-present `clusters.json` on disk (for the `tracker.adapter: "local"` config case).
3. Wire `polaris analyze spec <path>` in the CLI (extend the `analyze` or add a `spec` subcommand).
4. Wire `polaris run spec <path>` — reads the spec, calls `SpecAdapter.syncIn()`, saves the resulting `clusters.json`, then runs normal bootstrap.
5. Local child IDs use slugs derived from spec section headers, e.g. `spec-child-01`, `spec-child-02`.

## Scope

* src/tracker/adapters/spec/index.ts
* src/tracker/adapters/local/index.ts
* src/cli/index.ts
* src/cli/args.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `SpecAdapter.syncIn(path)` returns a valid `LocalGraph` from a spec markdown file.
* `polaris run spec <path>` bootstraps and dispatches children without requiring a tracker.
* Local child IDs are deterministic slugs (not UUIDs).
* `source.type` in the resulting graph is `"spec"`.
* Unit test for `SpecAdapter.syncIn()` with a fixture spec file passes.
* `npm run build` passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/tracker/adapters/spec

## Ordering

* Blocked by child 2 (source-agnostic graph loading must be in place first).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement GitHub issue intake in this child (prompt and GitHub sources are out of scope for this cluster).
* Do not modify finalize.

POL-321.title: Remove tracker-specific text from packet compiler and skill packet generator
POL-321.body:
## Objective

Replace all hardcoded "Linear" references in the packet compiler instruction text and skill packet generator output with tracker-conditional or tracker-agnostic language.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Two files contain hardcoded Linear text that will be wrong for trackerless runs:

1. `src/loop/worker-packet.ts:compileFinalizePacket()` — step 7 reads `"Update Linear parent issue ${input.clusterId} to Done."` — but finalize step 13 already has a `linearEnabled` guard. The instruction text should match the actual runtime behavior.
2. `src/skill-packet/generator.ts:buildAnalyzePacket()` — `allowed_outputs` contains `"Linear issue descriptions and child issues"` and `deliverables` contains `"Implementation-ready Linear issues, or"` — these are wrong for trackerless or GitHub-issue-backed analyze sessions.

## Goal

1. In `compileFinalizePacket()`, change step 7 to: `"If tracker is configured: update the parent issue ${input.clusterId} to In Review / Done per your tracker adapter. If no tracker: skip this step."` — or make it conditional on a `trackerEnabled` input flag passed to the compiler.
2. In `buildAnalyzePacket()`, change `allowed_outputs` and `deliverables` to use tracker-agnostic language: `"Tracker child issues or local work unit files (depending on work source)"` and `"Implementation-ready work units (tracker issues or local work unit files)"`.

## Scope

* src/loop/worker-packet.ts
* src/skill-packet/generator.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `compileFinalizePacket()` step 7 contains no hardcoded `"Linear"` string.
* `buildAnalyzePacket()` `allowed_outputs` and `deliverables` contain no hardcoded `"Linear"` string.
* All existing tests for these files pass.
* `npm run build` passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/worker-packet
* npx vitest run src/skill-packet/generator

## Ordering

* Blocked by the first child (WorkContract types must exist before this runs).

## Non-goals

* Do not change the packet structure or return contracts.
* Do not modify finalize runtime behavior (that is handled in a separate child).

POL-320.title: Replace LinearAdapter direct fallback in run-preflight with source-agnostic graph loading
POL-320.body:
## Objective

Remove the direct LinearAdapter import and fallback from `loadOrSyncGraph()` in `run-preflight.ts`, replacing it with a source-agnostic graph loading path.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). `src/loop/run-preflight.ts:loadOrSyncGraph()` currently imports `LinearAdapter` directly and falls back to `LinearAdapter.syncIn(clusterId)` when no local graph exists. This means `polaris run` silently requires Linear when no local `clusters.json` is present, even if the user is operating without a tracker. The fix replaces the hard-coded fallback with an adapter-dispatched or error-reporting path.

## Goal

Refactor `loadOrSyncGraph()` to:

1. If local `clusters.json` exists for `clusterId`: load and return it (no change from current behavior).
2. If it does not exist and `config.tracker?.adapter === "linear"` and `config.tracker.linear.enabled`: sync from Linear (preserve existing behavior).
3. If it does not exist and `config.tracker?.adapter === "mcp-bridge"`: use McpBridgeAdapter (preserve existing behavior where applicable).
4. If it does not exist and no tracker is configured (or `adapter === "local"`): throw a clear error: `"No local cluster graph found for ${clusterId}. Create one with: polaris tracker sync-in <id>, or bootstrap directly with: polaris loop bootstrap --cluster-id <id> --children <csv>"`.

Remove the static `LinearAdapter` import from `run-preflight.ts`.

## Scope

* src/loop/run-preflight.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `src/loop/run-preflight.ts` does not import `LinearAdapter` directly.
* `loadOrSyncGraph()` dispatches to the correct adapter based on `config.tracker.adapter`.
* Error message when no tracker is configured is clear and lists non-Linear options.
* Existing tests for `run-preflight.ts` pass.
* `npm run build` passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop/run-preflight.ts

## Ordering

* Blocked by the first child (WorkContract types must exist before this runs).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement the SpecAdapter or LocalAdapter in this child.
* Do not change bootstrap or dispatch logic.

POL-319.title: Define WorkContract type and extend PolarisConfig for multi-source support
POL-319.body:
## Objective

Introduce the normalized WorkContract interface and extend PolarisConfig to express work source type, enabling all downstream subsystems to operate source-agnostically.

## Context

Part of [POL-318](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-318/implement-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Before any other decoupling work can proceed, the shared type contract must exist. Currently there is no WorkContract type anywhere in the codebase — work flows from Linear IDs directly into LoopState and packet compilers with no normalization layer. This child establishes the foundation.

## Goal

Create `src/types/work-contract.ts` with the `WorkContract` interface matching the shape proposed in [POL-317](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-317/analyze-decouple-packets-from-tracker-assumptions-with-spec-to-work). Extend `PolarisConfig` in `src/config/schema.ts` to add a `work_source` field and add `"local"` to the `tracker.adapter` union. Update `src/config/defaults.ts` accordingly.

## Scope

* src/types/work-contract.ts
* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/defaults.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `WorkContract` interface is exported from `src/types/work-contract.ts` with fields: `source.type`, `source.id`, `source.path`, `source.url`, `objective`, `acceptance_criteria`, `allowed_scope`, `validation_commands`, `linked_docs`, `evidence_requirements`, `children`.
* `PolarisConfig.tracker.adapter` union includes `"local"`.
* `PolarisConfig` has an optional `work_source` field with `type` discriminant.
* `npm run build` passes with no type errors.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

None — this is the first child.

## Non-goals

* Do not wire up any CLI commands in this child.
* Do not modify any loop, finalize, or skill-packet files.

POL-317.title: ANALYZE: Decouple packets from tracker assumptions with spec-to-work-contract intake
